engineering_rules: SW pipes routed at z=4800mm (highway level) to stay above engine/gearbox maintenance envelope (z<2500mm)
Minimum 300mm separation maintained from HFO/DO fuel lines throughout
No SW pipe routed directly above main switchboard or soft-starter panels (DG electrical zones at x>10000)
DG branches use flexible hose connections to absorb engine vibration — prevents pipe fatigue
Emergency cross-connect (RT_XCONNECT_FER_AER) added to schematic which had no cross-connection — critical for damage control
Return lines routed starboard at z=4600mm (100mm below supply highway) to maintain visual separation
Non-return valves on all pump discharges to prevent reverse rotation on standby units
RO plant feed branched from suction side (pre-pump) with self-cleaning strainer — prevents membrane fouling
All bulkhead penetrations require A60 fire-rated glands per SOLAS II-2
CuNi 90/10 selected for all wetted SW surfaces; GRP permitted on suction > DN350 for weight saving

routes.description: Seawater from sea chest to pump suctions — FER
Branch → SWC_P1B
Branch → SWC_P3
Seawater from sea chest to pump suctions — AER
Branch → SWC_P2B
Branch → SWC_P4
SWC pump discharge to ME Coolant HE — FER
SWC pump discharge to ME LO HE — FER
ME Coolant HE SW outlet to overboard — FER
SWC pump discharge to ME Coolant HE — AER (mirror of FER)
SWC pump discharge to ME LO HE — AER
ME HE SW outlets to overboard — AER
SWC supply header to DG1-3 — common starboard header
Branch → DG1
Branch → DG2
Branch → DG3
SWC supply header to DG4-6 — AER
Branch → DG4
Branch → DG5
Branch → DG6
DG1-3 SW outlet collected and discharged overboard — FER
SWC supply to AC Plant 1&2 condenser
SWC supply to AC Plant 3&4 condenser
Seawater feed to RO Plant 1 & 2 (pre-filtered branch)
Emergency cross-connect: FER SWC pumps can serve AER HEs and vice versa
